steps.title: Use the tense anchor
steps.content: The previous sentence says economists in India use an Ease of Living Index. That simple present verb sets up the blank as a description of what the tool generally does now. The sentence is not describing an action completed before a past event, an unreal condition, or something continuing up to a future point.

steps.title: Choose the matching tense
steps.content: Choice A - measures. This is simple present tense, so it fits the current, general function of the tool. Choice B - had measured. This is past perfect and would imply the measuring happened before some later past event. Choice C - would have measured. This creates a hypothetical conditional meaning not supported by the sentence. Choice D - will have been measuring. This shifts the statement into future perfect continuous tense without any future time marker. The answer is A.
